mnemonics.title: BFS: Breadth First Search
mnemonics.content: BFS = Bank FIFO Service - Just like a bank serves customers in order of arrival, BFS visits all neighbors at current depth before moving deeper

mnemonics.title: DFS: Depth First Search
mnemonics.content: DFS = Drive Fast Straight - Like driving on a highway, going as deep as possible before taking exits

mnemonics.title: Topological Sort Order
mnemonics.content: Uphill Hike Rule: Think of topological sort as hiking up a mountain - you can only step on peaks (nodes with no incoming edges from unscheduled nodes)

mnemonics.title: Kahn's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: KAHN = Keep Adding Heroes' Nodes - 1) Calculate In-degrees, 2) Add zero in-degree nodes to queue, 3) Remove/process nodes, 4) Reduce neighbor in-degrees

mnemonics.title: DFS Topological Sort
mnemonics.content: LION Method: Last In, Out First - Perform DFS, record nodes in reverse order of finish times (push to stack when finished)

mnemonics.title: DAG Memory Hook
mnemonics.content: DAG = Directions Allow Growth - Acyclic means no cycles, like a one-way street that doesn't loop back

acronyms.usage: Use when finding shortest path in unweighted graphs, level-order traversal
Use for cycle detection, topological sort, exploring deep paths, maze solving
Only DAGs can have topological ordering; if graph has cycle, no topological sort exists
Number of edges pointing TO v; nodes with indegree 0 are starting points in topological sort
Queue property used in BFS - first node discovered is first node processed
Stack property used in DFS - most recently discovered node is processed next

visualTips.visualization: Imagine ripples in a pond - start at center (source), waves expand outward uniformly layer by layer
Think of a maze exploration where you keep your hand on the right wall and go as far as possible before backtracking
Picture a staircase where each step represents a task - you can only stand on steps (nodes) whose supporting steps (prerequisites) are already completed
Visualize removing leaves from a tree - nodes with zero indegree are 'leaves', remove them, then new leaves appear
Imagine a snake trying to bite its own tail - if during DFS you encounter a node still in current recursion stack, a cycle exists
Count arrows pointing INTO a node like counting doors entering a room - more doors = higher indegree

analogies: Like exploring a city: BFS is like checking all streets in your current block before moving to the next block; DFS is like taking one street as far as possible before trying another

analogies.analogy: Planning semester subjects: You must complete 'Introduction to Programming' before 'Data Structures' - this ordering of courses follows topological sort
Building a house: You must complete foundation before walls, walls before roof - these tasks form a DAG with topological order
Indegree is like receiving calls (incoming connections), Outdegree is like making calls (outgoing connections)
Queue at movie ticket counter - people form a line, first person in line gets served first, new people join at the back
Stack of plates in a cafeteria - last plate placed on top is first one removed; DFS uses this Last-In-First-Out behavior
